manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|>human: Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet. <sep> assistant:

<|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|>human: Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet. <sep> assistant: <reasoning>
Let's tackle this clue-giving problem. The user's team has words: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post. I need to find a single-word clue that relates to as many of these as possible without being one of the words or containing parts of them.

First, let's look for common themes. Spine, line, post, string, ground, train, missile, moon, luck. Hmm. Maybe something related to structure? Spine is part of the body, but also a structure. Post and line could be structural. Train has lines. String is a line. Ground is a base. Missile might be a stretch. Moon and luck don't fit here. Wait, maybe "rail" or "track"? Train has tracks, line could be a rail line. Post might be a rail post. But rail isn't in the list. Alternatively, "track" could relate to train, line, maybe string as a track? Not sure. 

Another angle: "string" and "line" are both linear. "Post" and "spine" are structural supports. "Train" moves on tracks. "Missile" could be a stretch. "Ground" is a base. Maybe "support"? Spine, post, ground, train tracks? Not sure. 

Wait, "rail" is part of a train track. So train, line (as in rail line), post (as in rail post), string (maybe a string as a thin rail?). Not quite. Alternatively, "track" could be a clue for train, line, maybe string as a track? Not sure. 

Alternatively, think of "axis". Spine is an axis, moon orbits around an axis. But that's two words. Maybe "axis" relates to spine and moon. Not enough. 

What about "railroad"? But that's multiple words. The clue has to be single-word. 

Wait, "line" is already a target. Maybe "rail" is too close to "line"? Not sure. 

Another thought: "spine" is part of a book, "line" is a line in a book, "string" could be a thread in a book? Maybe not. 

Wait, "post" and "spine" could be parts of a fence or structure. "Missile" is a projectile. Maybe "target"? Missile is a target
